recent_excerpts: Threat actors are actively exploiting three recently leaked Windows Defender privilege escalation vulnerabilities, using public proof-of-concept code to gain SYSTEM privileges on enterprise systems.
A security researcher has published proof-of-concept exploits for two new zero-day flaws in Microsoft Defender, dubbed RedSun and UnDefend.
Threat actors are actively exploiting three zero-day vulnerabilities in Microsoft Defender, codenamed BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, to gain SYSTEM privileges.
